title: nlmixr2lib — Claude project notes
text: file gives AI coding assistants fast orientation repository. Human contributors read vignettes/create-model-library.Rmd full conventions README project overview.

title: nlmixr2lib — Claude project notes
text: nlmixr2lib model library nlmixr2. ships curated pharmacometric models (PK, PD, endogenous, therapeutic-area-specific) users can load name combine modify.

title: nlmixr2lib — Claude project notes
text: inst/modeldb/ — every distributed model, organized category (specificDrugs/, endogenous/, pharmacokinetics/, pharmacodynamics/, therapeuticArea/). function name inside .R file must match filename. R/modeldb.R — registry-building machinery (buildModelDb(), readModelDb(), modellib()). vignettes/ — validation vignettes, one per published model. vignettes/create-model-library.Rmd — canonical naming conventions document.

title: nlmixr2lib — Claude project notes
text: Repo-scoped skills live .claude/skills/: extract-literature-model — guided workflow extracting pharmacometric model scientific source (journal article, supplement, poster, regulatory document) package. Use user provides paper asks add model. See .claude/skills/extract-literature-model/SKILL.md. skill’s references/ folder contains templates standards enforces. authoritative covariate-column register .claude/skills/extract-literature-model/references/covariate-columns.md — consult introducing new covariate column.

title: nlmixr2lib — Claude project notes
text: full details see vignettes/create-model-library.Rmd .claude/skills/extract-literature-model/references/naming-conventions.md. Compartments: depot, central, peripheral1, peripheral2, effect. Observation: Cc. PK parameters (log-scale): lka, lcl, lvc, lvp, lq, lfdepot; derived ka, cl, vc, vp, q, kel, k12, etc. IIV: eta + transformed name (etalcl, etalvc). Use etalcl even paper used etacl. Residual error: propSd, addSd; multi-output prefixes output (CcpropSd). Covariate columns: canonical names covariate-columns.md. Standardized choices include SEXF (1 = female), ADA_POS (1 = ADA-positive), RACE_<GROUP> prefix race indicators.

title: nlmixr2lib — Claude project notes
text: Never push directly main. Always branch open PR. regenerating data/modeldb.rda / inst/modeldb.qs2, sync origin/main upstream additions aren’t clobbered. nlmixr2lib::buildModelDb() regenerates registry; commit regenerated artifacts alongside model file vignette.

title: nlmixr2lib — Claude project notes
text: devtools::check() must pass. Model files validated via buildModelDb() (filename ↔︎ function-name match, parseable ini() / model()). Vignettes must build cleanly; use PKNCA (Suggests) NCA validation.

title: CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide
text: Citation: Carlsson Petri KC, Hale PM, Hesse D, Rathor N, Mastrandrea LD. Liraglutide pharmacokinetics exposure-response adolescents obesity. Pediatric Obesity. 2021;16(10):e12799. doi:10.1111/ijpo.12799 Description: Liraglutide PK model adolescents (Carlsson Petri 2021) Article: Pediatric Obesity 2021;16(10):e12799 Open-access PMC copy: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC8519033/

title: CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide
text: published analysis pooled 176 subjects across four Novo Nordisk trials liraglutide (Saxenda, 3.0 mg maintenance -daily subcutaneous): 121 adolescents phase 3a trial NN2211-4180 (primary population), 13 adolescents phase 1 trial NN2211-3967, 13 children NN2211-4181, 29 adults NN2211-3630 (Carlsson Petri 2021 Methods). adolescent phase 3a population (Table 1) 55.4% female (67/121), 84.3% White / 10.7% Black / 1.7% Asian / 3.3% , mean body weight 99.4 kg (SD 19.7, range 62.1-178.2 kg) mean age 14.6 years (SD 1.6, range 12-17). 107/121 adolescents reached 3.0 mg maintenance dose weekly escalation (0.6-1.2-1.8-2.4-3.0 mg). Age cutoffs used define CHILD / ADOLESCENT indicators children 7-11 y, adolescents 12-17 y, adults >=18 y. information available programmatically via readModelDb("CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide")$population.

title: CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide
text: Per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide.R. table collects review.

title: CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide
text: Original observed data publicly available. cohort approximates Carlsson Petri 2021 Table 1 adolescent phase 3a (trial NN2211-4180) demographics: 55.4% female, body weight ~ Normal(99.4, 19.7) truncated reported 62.1-178.2 kg range, CHILD = 0 ADOLESCENT = 1. subjects receive 3.0 mg -daily SC maintenance dose (escalation, match 107/121 “maintenance” regimen). Liraglutide molar mass 3751.2 g/mol (C172H265N43O51); doses entered nmol simulated Cc = central / Vc directly nmol/L (matching paper’s reported concentration units). event table -daily SC dosing four weeks (sampling every hour last dosing interval capture steady-state shape) constructed .
code: set.seed(20210609) # publication date reference n_subj <- 400  # Liraglutide molar mass (g/mol). 3 mg = 3e-3 / 3751.2 mol = 7.997e-7 mol = 799.7 nmol. lira_mw <- 3751.2 dose_mg <- 3.0 dose_nmol <- dose_mg * 1e6 / lira_mw # convert mg to nmol: 1 mg = (1 / MW) * 1e6 nmol  cohort <- tibble(   id          = seq_len(n_subj),   SEXF        = as.integer(runif(n_subj) < 0.554),   WT          = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 99.4, sd = 19.7), 62.1), 178.2),   CHILD       = 0L,   ADOLESCENT  = 1L,   treatment   = factor("3 mg QD (adolescent)") ) sim_days  <- 28 tau       <- 24                       # dosing interval (h) n_doses   <- sim_days                 # one dose per day dose_times <- seq(0, by = tau, length.out = n_doses)  # Dense sampling on the final dosing interval (for Cmax / Tmax / AUCtau), # plus coarse sampling earlier (for visual steady-state approach). final_dose_time <- dose_times[n_doses] obs_times <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, final_dose_time, by = 6),                 # every 6 h across the run-in   final_dose_time + c(0, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 12, 16, 20, 24) )))  dose_rows <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(time = dose_times) |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = dose_nmol, cmt = "depot", evid = 1L)  obs_rows <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(time = obs_times) |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L)  events <- dplyr::bind_rows(dose_rows, obs_rows) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, cmt, evid, SEXF, WT, CHILD, ADOLESCENT, treatment) |>   dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid))

title: CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide
code: mod <- rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide")) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(   mod, events = events,   keep = c("SEXF", "WT", "CHILD", "ADOLESCENT", "treatment") )

what: Typical steady-state concentration profile over the last 24 h
title: CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide
text: Carlsson Petri 2021 publish concentration-time figure 3.0 mg adolescent dose, report exposure metric Cavg = dose / (CL/F * 24 h) steady state. deterministic (“typical”) profile zeros random effects median trajectory reference 100 kg female adolescent visible.
code: mod_typical <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe()  typical_cohort <- tibble(   id = 1,   SEXF = 1L, WT = 100, CHILD = 0L, ADOLESCENT = 1L,   treatment = factor("Typical adolescent, 100 kg female, 3 mg QD") )  typical_doses <- typical_cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(time = dose_times) |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = dose_nmol, cmt = "depot", evid = 1L)  typical_obs <- typical_cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(time = seq(0, final_dose_time + tau, by = 0.5)) |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L)  typical_events <- dplyr::bind_rows(typical_doses, typical_obs) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, cmt, evid, SEXF, WT, CHILD, ADOLESCENT, treatment) |>   dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid))  sim_typical <- rxode2::rxSolve(   mod_typical, events = typical_events,   keep = c("SEXF", "WT", "CHILD", "ADOLESCENT", "treatment") ) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc'  sim_typical |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc)) |>   ggplot(aes(time / 24, Cc)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   labs(x = "Time (days)", y = "Liraglutide concentration (nmol/L)",        title = "Typical adolescent: 3 mg QD SC, 100 kg female",        caption = "Deterministic typical-value prediction.") +   theme_minimal()

what: VPC-style median and 5-95 percentiles over the 28-day run-in
title: CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide
text: stochastic cohort summary (median 5th 95th percentiles across 400 simulated adolescents) replicates type summary shown paper’s Figure 3 (exposure metrics vs. body weight / age group).
code: sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time > 0) |>   dplyr::group_by(time, treatment) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   ggplot(aes(time / 24, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line() +   labs(x = "Time (days)", y = "Liraglutide concentration (nmol/L)",        title = "Simulated 5-50-95 percentiles across adolescent cohort (3 mg QD SC)",        caption = "Cohort covariates approximate Carlsson Petri 2021 Table 1 (trial NN2211-4180).")

what: Exposure vs. body weight at steady state
title: CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide
text: Carlsson Petri 2021 Figure 3A shows individual steady-state average concentration decreases increasing body weight (consistent positive allometric exponents CL V, greater effect CL). replicate relationship computing simulated subject’s Cavg final dosing interval.
code: cavg_by_id <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time >= final_dose_time, time <= final_dose_time + tau,                 !is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::group_by(id, WT, SEXF) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Cavg_nmol_L = mean(Cc),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   dplyr::mutate(Sex = ifelse(SEXF == 1L, "Female", "Male"))  ggplot(cavg_by_id, aes(WT, Cavg_nmol_L, colour = Sex)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +   geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE) +   labs(x = "Body weight (kg)", y = "C_avg,ss (nmol/L)",        title = "Steady-state Cavg vs. body weight",        caption = "Replicates the shape of Carlsson Petri 2021 Figure 3A (adolescents, 3 mg QD).") +   theme_minimal() #> `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

title: CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide
text: Compute Cmax, Tmax, C_tau, C_avg, AUClast using PKNCA final dosing interval steady state (recipe 3). treatment grouping variable (treatment) placed id formula per project convention. Steady-state NCA final 24-h dosing interval (3 mg QD SC adolescent cohort).
code: sim_nca <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time >= final_dose_time, time <= final_dose_time + tau) |>   dplyr::mutate(time_rel = time - final_dose_time) |>   dplyr::select(id, time = time_rel, Cc, treatment)  dose_df <- events |>   dplyr::filter(evid == 1, time == final_dose_time) |>   dplyr::mutate(time = 0) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start    = 0,   end      = tau,   cmax     = TRUE,   tmax     = TRUE,   cmin     = TRUE,   auclast  = TRUE,   cav      = TRUE )  nca_data <- PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) nca_res  <- PKNCA::pk.nca(nca_data) #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■                     40% |  ETA:  2s knitr::kable(summary(nca_res),              caption = "Steady-state NCA at the final 24-h dosing interval (3 mg QD SC adolescent cohort).")

title: CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide
text: Carlsson Petri 2021 reports steady-state Cavg = dose / (CL/F * 24) individual (Figure 3A), tabulated population mean; however, reference female adult (CL/F typical = 1.01 L/h), predicted Cavg 3.0 mg dose : Cavg_ref = 3 mg / (1.01 L/h * 24 h) = 0.1238 mg/L = 33.0 nmol/L (liraglutide MW = 3751.2 g/mol) typical 100 kg female adolescent, model adds 1.06x age factor CL (adolescent/adult), typical CL = 1.01 * (100/100)^0.762 * 1.12^0 * 1.06 = 1.0706 L/h, giving predicted typical Cavg 3 / (1.0706 * 24) * 1e6 / 3751.2 = 31.1 nmol/L. Predicted steady-state Cavg 3 mg QD adolescent cohort.
code: typical_cl <- 1.01 * (100/100)^0.762 * 1.12^(1 - 1) * 1.06^1 # female (SEXF=1), 100 kg, ADOLESCENT typical_cavg_mg_L <- dose_mg / (typical_cl * 24) typical_cavg_nmol_L <- typical_cavg_mg_L * 1e6 / lira_mw  # Median simulated Cavg from the stochastic cohort sim_cavg <- cavg_by_id |>   dplyr::summarise(median_Cavg_nmol_L = median(Cavg_nmol_L),                    q05 = quantile(Cavg_nmol_L, 0.05),                    q95 = quantile(Cavg_nmol_L, 0.95))  compare_tbl <- tibble::tibble(   Source      = c("Typical-value closed form",                   "Simulated cohort (median)",                   "Simulated cohort (90% range)"),   Cavg_nmol_L = c(sprintf("%.1f", typical_cavg_nmol_L),                   sprintf("%.1f", sim_cavg$median_Cavg_nmol_L),                   sprintf("%.1f - %.1f", sim_cavg$q05, sim_cavg$q95)) )  knitr::kable(compare_tbl,              caption = "Predicted steady-state Cavg for the 3 mg QD adolescent cohort.")

title: CarlssonPetri_2021_liraglutide
text: published analysis covariate SEXM (1 = male, 0 = female) translated canonical SEXF (1 = female). sex effect CL encoded e_sex_cl^(1 - SEXF) males retain 1.12x factor Table 3 females reference (factor = 1). previous implementation (1 - SEXF)^e_sex_cl bug: females (SEXF = 1), 0^1.12 = 0 zeroed CL. 100, correct transformation isomega^2 = log(1 + CV^2). fixed model file; see -file comment onetalcl/etalvc`. age-effect encoding previously CHILD^e_age_child_cl *   ADOLESCENT^e_age_adolescent_cl, evaluates 0^1.11 * 0^1.06 = 0 adults (indicators 0). corrected form e_age_child_cl^CHILD * e_age_adolescent_cl^ADOLESCENT, correctly evaluates 1 adults, 1.06 adolescents, 1.11 children. simulated cohort uses NN2211-4180 adolescent phase 3a demographics (weight, sex). Race/ethnicity model covariate therefore reproduced. simulated subjects “adolescents 3.0 mg maintenance” match dominant 107/121 regimen; weekly dose escalation reproduced paper’s steady-state exposure metric concerns maintenance dose. Liraglutide molar mass (3751.2 g/mol) used convert mg dose input nmol amount needed Cc nmol/L. source paper’s LLOQ 0.03 nmol/L. deterministic typical-value check agrees closed-form Cavg = dose / (CL/F * 24) within rounding; stochastic cohort median tracks typical-value prediction within ~10% across sampled weight distribution.

title: Cirincione_2017_exenatide
text: Citation: Cirincione B, Mager DE. Population pharmacokinetics exenatide. British Journal Clinical Pharmacology. 2017;83(3):517-526. doi:10.1111/bcp.13135 Description: Population PK model exenatide immediate-release (Cirincione 2017): two-compartment, parallel linear Michaelis-Menten elimination, sequential zero-order saturable first-order absorption SC dosing. Article: Br J Clin Pharmacol. 2017;83(3):517-526

title: Cirincione_2017_exenatide
text: published analysis pooled 195 subjects 8 clinical trials, including healthy volunteers patients type 2 diabetes mellitus. Baseline demographics Cirincione 2017 Table 1: 22% female, 67% Caucasian; age 18-76 years (mean 48, SD 12.9); body weight 52.6-162 kg (mean 86, SD 16.2); MDRD eGFR ranged 4.1 mL/min/1.73 m^2 (ESRD) >= 90 (normal), pooled mean 88.7 (SD 31.4). Renal-function distribution: 129 normal, 47 mild impairment, 10 moderate, 1 severe, 8 ESRD. Single repeated SC doses 2.5-20 ug. information available programmatically via readModelDb("Cirincione_2017_exenatide")$population.

title: Cirincione_2017_exenatide
text: Per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/specificDrugs/Cirincione_2017_exenatide.R. table collects review.

title: Cirincione_2017_exenatide
text: Original observed data publicly available. cohort approximates Table 1 demographics: body weight truncated normal centered 86 kg (SD 16.2), eGFR distribution matching reported renal-function bands. non-Study-1 / non-Study-5 (pooled “”) residual group simulated NCA comparison uses largest share original cohort. fixed 10 ug single SC dose used match paper’s Figure 5 reference dose.
code: set.seed(20260418) n_subj <- 500  renal_bands <- tibble::tibble(   band       = c("normal", "mild", "moderate", "severe", "ESRD"),   n_in_paper = c(129, 47, 10, 1, 8),   egfr_min   = c(90, 60, 30, 15, 4.1),   egfr_max   = c(140, 89, 59, 29, 14) ) renal_bands$frac <- renal_bands$n_in_paper / sum(renal_bands$n_in_paper)  sample_band <- sample(renal_bands$band, n_subj, replace = TRUE, prob = renal_bands$frac) egfr_draws <- vapply(sample_band, function(b) {   row <- renal_bands[renal_bands$band == b, ]   runif(1, row$egfr_min, row$egfr_max) }, numeric(1))  cohort <- tibble(   id     = seq_len(n_subj),   WT     = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 86, sd = 16.2), 52.6), 162),   eGFR   = egfr_draws,   STUDY1 = 0L,   STUDY5 = 0L,   treatment = factor(sample_band, levels = c("normal", "mild", "moderate", "severe", "ESRD")) )  events <- cohort |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = 10, cmt = "depot", evid = 1L, time = 0) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, cmt, evid, WT, eGFR, STUDY1, STUDY5, treatment) |>   dplyr::bind_rows(     cohort |>       tidyr::crossing(time = c(seq(0, 4, 0.1), seq(4.2, 12, 0.2))) |>       dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L) |>       dplyr::select(id, time, amt, cmt, evid, WT, eGFR, STUDY1, STUDY5, treatment)   ) |>   dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid))

title: Cirincione_2017_exenatide
text: deterministic replication (typical-value predictions without -subject variability, matching paper’s Figure 5):
code: mod <- rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("Cirincione_2017_exenatide")) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events, keep = c("WT", "eGFR", "treatment")) mod_typical <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe() #> Warning: No sigma parameters in the model  typical_scenarios <- tibble::tibble(   id       = 1:6,   scenario = c("75 kg, eGFR 90", "100 kg, eGFR 90", "125 kg, eGFR 90",                "75 kg, eGFR 75", "75 kg, eGFR 45", "median (84.8 kg, eGFR 80)"),   WT       = c(75, 100, 125, 75, 75, 84.8),   eGFR     = c(90,  90,  90, 75, 45, 80),   STUDY1   = 0L,   STUDY5   = 0L )  ev_typical <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_len(nrow(typical_scenarios)), function(i) {   ev_i <- rxode2::et(amt = 10, cmt = "depot") |>     rxode2::et(seq(0, 12, 0.05)) |>     as.data.frame()   ev_i$id <- typical_scenarios$id[i]   ev_i }))  sim_typical <- rxode2::rxSolve(   mod_typical, events = ev_typical,   params = typical_scenarios[, c("id", "WT", "eGFR", "STUDY1", "STUDY5")],   returnType = "tibble" ) |>   dplyr::left_join(typical_scenarios[, c("id", "scenario")], by = "id") #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalkm' #> Warning: multi-subject simulation without without 'omega'

what: Figure 5 — typical concentration by body weight and eGFR
title: Cirincione_2017_exenatide
text: Cirincione 2017 Figure 5 shows simulated typical concentration-time profiles 10 ug SC dose across body weight eGFR scenarios. Lower body weight lower eGFR increase exposure (lower central volume lower linear clearance, respectively).
code: sim_typical |>   ggplot(aes(time, Cc, colour = scenario)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   labs(x = "Time (h)", y = "Exenatide concentration (pg/mL)",        colour = NULL,        title = "Typical concentration profiles (replicates Figure 5)",        caption = "Replicates Figure 5 of Cirincione 2017: 10 ug SC, typical values.") +   theme_minimal() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: Cirincione_2017_exenatide
text: VPC-style summary simulated cohort, stratified renal-function band.
code: sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time > 0) |>   dplyr::group_by(time, treatment) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   ggplot(aes(time, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.2) +   geom_line() +   facet_wrap(~treatment) +   labs(x = "Time (h)", y = "Exenatide concentration (pg/mL)",        title = "Simulated 5-50-95 percentiles by renal-function band",        caption = "10 ug SC; cohort covariate distributions approximate Table 1.") #> `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation. #> ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic?

title: Cirincione_2017_exenatide
text: Compute Cmax, Tmax, AUC0-last, terminal half-life using PKNCA. Groups renal-function band (treatment stratifier) summary can compared paper’s typical values.
code: sim_nca <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time > 0) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, Cc, treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca, Cc ~ time | treatment + id)  dose_df <- events |>   dplyr::filter(evid == 1) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, treatment)  dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start      = 0,   end        = 12,   cmax       = TRUE,   tmax       = TRUE,   auclast    = TRUE,   half.life  = TRUE )  nca_res <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals)) #> Warning: Requesting an
