title: Cirincione_2017_exenatide
text: paper reports typical Cmax 75 kg approximately 287, 273, 321 pg/mL eGFR 90, 75, 45 mL/min/1.73 m^2, respectively (Figure 5), typical terminal half-life ~2.4 h. deterministic (“typical”) check simulated cohort: Simulated typical values (cmax, tmax, half.life) vs. Cirincione 2017 Figure 5 / typical half-life.
code: nca_typical <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(   PKNCA::PKNCAconc(     sim_typical |>       dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time > 0) |>       dplyr::mutate(treatment = scenario) |>       dplyr::distinct(id, time, treatment, .keep_all = TRUE) |>       dplyr::select(id, time, Cc, treatment),     Cc ~ time | treatment + id   ),   PKNCA::PKNCAdose(     typical_scenarios |>       dplyr::mutate(time = 0, amt = 10, treatment = scenario) |>       dplyr::select(id, time, amt, treatment),     amt ~ time | treatment + id   ),   intervals = data.frame(start = 0, end = 12, cmax = TRUE, tmax = TRUE, half.life = TRUE) ))  nca_typical_tbl <- as.data.frame(nca_typical$result) |>   dplyr::filter(PPTESTCD %in% c("cmax", "tmax", "half.life")) |>   dplyr::select(treatment, PPTESTCD, PPORRES) |>   tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = PPTESTCD, values_from = PPORRES)  published <- tibble::tribble(   ~treatment,                     ~cmax_pub, ~thalf_pub,   "75 kg, eGFR 90",                  287,       2.4,   "100 kg, eGFR 90",                 237,       2.4,   "125 kg, eGFR 90",                 189,       2.4,   "75 kg, eGFR 75",                  273,       2.4,   "75 kg, eGFR 45",                  321,       2.4,   "median (84.8 kg, eGFR 80)",        NA,       2.4 )  dplyr::left_join(published, nca_typical_tbl, by = "treatment") |>   knitr::kable(digits = 2,                caption = "Simulated typical values (cmax, tmax, half.life) vs. Cirincione 2017 Figure 5 / typical half-life.")

title: Cirincione_2017_exenatide
text: source column DVID (character-valued: "study1" / "study5" / "otherStudy") split two binary indicators (STUDY1, STUDY5) per nlmixr2lib covariate-columns.md register. STUDY1 = STUDY5 = 0 selects pooled-residual error. Km reported paper 567 pg/mL. model encoded ng/mL (0.567) remain unit-consistent Cc = central / vc (ug/L = ng/mL); observation subsequently multiplied 1000 report pg/mL. virtual cohort approximates Table 1 demographics. Race distribution used model therefore reproduced. eGFR sampled uniformly within renal-function band using paper’s per-band counts. IIV variance encoded log(1 + CV^2) (log-normal variance CV) rather shorthand log(1 + CV) prior version file used. matches standard NONMEM → nlmixr2 interpretation paper reports CV% log-normal random effects. Typical-value Cmax agrees Figure 5 within ~10% across weight eGFR scenarios; simulated terminal half-life ~2.4 h matches published value. Differences corrected tuning parameters.

title: Clegg_2024_nirsevimab
text: Replicate Figures 4, 5, 6 Clegg et al. (2024): Figure 4: Prediction-corrected VPC final model, stratified trial RSV season (Phase 2b, MELODY, MEDLEY Season 1, MEDLEY Season 2). Figure 5: Prediction-corrected VPC MELODY safety cohort. Figure 6: Predicted exposure (AUC₀₋₃₆₅, C_max, Day-151 concentration) versus body weight gestational age (GA) group weight-banded dosing. original study data publicly available, figures show simulated prediction intervals virtual infant populations whose covariate distributions approximate published trial demographics (Table 1, Clegg 2024). Body weight trajectories generated using weight--age growth standards (combined sex, 0–24 months).

title: Clegg_2024_nirsevimab
text: weight--age LMS parameters combined sex, 0–24 months (Multicentre Growth Reference Study Group, 2006). Formula: weight (kg) = M × (1 + L × S × z)^(1/L)

what: Helper: build a virtual cohort dataset
title: Clegg_2024_nirsevimab
text: Builds dose + observation records virtual cohort n infants. ga_range: gestational age range weeks. pna0_range: postnatal age dosing range months. max_day: follow-duration days. season2: TRUE Season 2 (200 mg flat dose, Season 2 CL effect applied). obs_days: observation time points days.
code: make_cohort <- function(n, ga_range, pna0_range, max_day,                         season2 = FALSE, obs_days = seq(0, max_day, by = 7)) {   GA    <- runif(n, ga_range[1], ga_range[2])   # weeks   PNA_0 <- runif(n, pna0_range[1], pna0_range[2]) # months at dosing   wt_z  <- pmax(-2, pmin(2, rnorm(n, 0, 1)))   WT_0  <- who_weight(PNA_0, wt_z)   AMT   <- if (season2) rep(200, n) else ifelse(WT_0 < 5, 50, 100)    pop <- data.frame(     ID = seq_len(n), GA, PNA_0, wt_z, WT_0, AMT,     SEASON2   = as.integer(season2),     ADA_POS   = 0L,     # All White/Nat.Haw reference race for simplicity     RACE_BLACK_OTH         = 0L,     RACE_ASIAN_AMIND_MULTI = 0L   )    # Dose records   d_dose <- pop |>     mutate(       TIME = 0, EVID = 1, CMT = "depot", DV = NA,       PAGE = GA / 4.35 + PNA_0,       WT   = WT_0     )    # Observation records with time-varying WT and PAGE   d_obs <- pop[rep(seq_len(n), each = length(obs_days)), ] |>     mutate(       TIME = rep(obs_days, times = n),       EVID = 0, CMT = "central", DV = NA, AMT = 0,       PNA  = PNA_0 + TIME / 30.44,       PAGE = GA / 4.35 + PNA,       WT   = who_weight(pmax(0, PNA), wt_z)     )    bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>     arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) |>     select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV, WT, PAGE,            RACE_BLACK_OTH, RACE_ASIAN_AMIND_MULTI, SEASON2, ADA_POS, WT_0) }

title: Clegg_2024_nirsevimab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Clegg_2024_nirsevimab")

what: Figure 4 — Prediction-corrected VPC by trial and season
title: Clegg_2024_nirsevimab
text: Four panels matching published trials: vignette shows simulated prediction intervals (observed data; original data publicly available).
code:
set.seed(8897)  # MEDI8897 = nirsevimab development code  d_p2b    <- make_cohort(200, c(29, 35), c(0, 3),  400) |> mutate(trial = "Phase 2b
(healthy infants, 29 to <35 wGA)") d_melody <- make_cohort(200, c(35, 42), c(0, 3),  500) |> mutate(trial = "MELODY
(healthy infants, ≥35 wGA)") d_med_s1 <- make_cohort(200, c(24, 35), c(0, 3),  400) |> mutate(trial = "MEDLEY Season 1
(infants ≤35 wGA, CHD/CLD)") d_med_s2 <- make_cohort(100, c(35, 42), c(12, 24), 400,                          season2 = TRUE)             |> mutate(trial = "MEDLEY Season 2
(children with CHD/CLD)")  sim_f4 <- bind_rows(d_p2b, d_melody, d_med_s1, d_med_s2)  out_f4 <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = sim_f4) |>   as.data.frame() |>   left_join(     sim_f4 |> select(ID, trial) |> distinct(),     by = c("id" = "ID")   ) #> Warning in left_join(as.data.frame(rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = sim_f4)), : Detected an unexpected many-to-many relationship between `x` and `y`. #> ℹ Row 1 of `x` matches multiple rows in `y`. #> ℹ Row 1 of `y` matches multiple rows in `x`. #> ℹ If a many-to-many relationship is expected, set `relationship = #>   "many-to-many"` to silence this warning. d_f4 <- out_f4 |>   group_by(trial, time) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   mutate(trial = factor(trial, levels = c(     "Phase 2b
(healthy infants, 29 to <35 wGA)",     "MELODY
(healthy infants, ≥35 wGA)",     "MEDLEY Season 1
(infants ≤35 wGA, CHD/CLD)",     "MEDLEY Season 2
(children with CHD/CLD)"   ))) ggplot(d_f4, aes(x = time, y = Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), fill = "#4682b4", alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line(colour = "#4682b4", linewidth = 0.8) +   facet_wrap(~trial, scales = "free_x", nrow = 2) +   scale_y_log10(     limits = c(0.1, NA),     labels = scales::label_number(drop0trailing = TRUE)   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 500, by = 100)) +   labs(     x     = "Time after dose (days)",     y     = "Nirsevimab serum concentration (μg/mL)
Prediction corrected",     title = "Figure 4 — Prediction-corrected VPC by trial and season",     caption = "Simulated median (line) and 90% prediction interval (shaded).
Original observed data not available; reference race = White, ADA negative."   ) +   theme_bw() +   theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 9)) #> Warning in scale_y_log10(limits = c(0.1, NA), labels = scales::label_number(drop0trailing = TRUE)): log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values.

what: Figure 5 — Prediction-corrected VPC for the MELODY safety cohort
title: Clegg_2024_nirsevimab
text: Healthy term late-preterm infants (≥35 weeks GA), Season 1, followed 360 days. paper uses 10th/50th/90th percentiles (rather 5th/95th used Figure 4).
code: set.seed(3979)  # NCT03979313 = MELODY trial d_f5 <- make_cohort(500, c(35, 42), c(0, 3), 360,                     obs_days = seq(0, 360, by = 7))  out_f5 <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = d_f5) |> as.data.frame() d_f5_plot <- out_f5 |>   group_by(time) |>   summarise(     Q10 = quantile(Cc, 0.10, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q90 = quantile(Cc, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) ggplot(d_f5_plot, aes(x = time, y = Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q10, ymax = Q90), fill = "#4682b4", alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line(colour = "#4682b4", linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_line(aes(y = Q10), linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.5) +   geom_line(aes(y = Q90), linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.5) +   scale_y_log10(     limits = c(0.5, NA),     labels = scales::label_number(drop0trailing = TRUE)   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 360, by = 30)) +   labs(     x     = "Time after dose (days)",     y     = "Nirsevimab serum concentration (\u03bcg/mL)\nPrediction corrected",     title = "Figure 5 \u2014 Prediction-corrected VPC: MELODY safety cohort",     caption = "Simulated 10th/50th/90th percentiles (N = 500 virtual infants, \u226535 wGA).\nOriginal observed data not available."   ) +   theme_bw() #> Warning in scale_y_log10(limits = c(0.5, NA), labels = scales::label_number(drop0trailing = TRUE)): log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values. #> log-10 transformation introduced infinite values.

what: Figure 6 — Predicted exposure versus body weight by GA group
title: Clegg_2024_nirsevimab
text: Simulate 3 GA groups (GA <29 weeks, ≥29 <35 weeks, ≥35 weeks), dosing birth, weight-banded dosing (50 mg ≤5 kg, 100 mg >5 kg). Compute AUC₀₋₃₆₅ (trapezoidal), C_max, Day-151 concentration. Use daily time steps accurate NCA. Adult reference lines paper (3000 mg IV dose): AUC₀₋₃₆₅ = 57.0 day·mg/mL; C_max = 1140 μg/mL. EC₉₀ (preclinical) = 6.8 μg/mL (Day-151 concentration panel).
code: set.seed(2024) n_per_ga <- 200 obs_daily <- seq(0, 365, by = 1)  ga_groups <- list(   list(label = "GA < 29 weeks",        ga = c(24, 29)),   list(label = "GA \u226529 to <35 weeks", ga = c(29, 35)),   list(label = "GA \u226535 weeks",        ga = c(35, 42)) )  sim_f6_list <- lapply(ga_groups, function(g) {   GA    <- runif(n_per_ga, g$ga[1], g$ga[2])   PNA_0 <- rep(0, n_per_ga)   # dose at birth   wt_z  <- pmax(-2, pmin(2, rnorm(n_per_ga, 0, 1)))   WT_0  <- who_weight(PNA_0, wt_z)   AMT   <- ifelse(WT_0 <= 5, 50, 100)    pop <- data.frame(     ID = seq_len(n_per_ga), GA, PNA_0, wt_z, WT_0, AMT,     SEASON2 = 0L, ADA_POS = 0L,     RACE_BLACK_OTH = 0L, RACE_ASIAN_AMIND_MULTI = 0L   )    d_dose <- pop |>     mutate(TIME = 0, EVID = 1, CMT = "depot", DV = NA,            PAGE = GA / 4.35, WT = WT_0)    d_obs <- pop[rep(seq_len(n_per_ga), each = length(obs_daily)), ] |>     mutate(       TIME = rep(obs_daily, times = n_per_ga),       EVID = 0, CMT = "central", DV = NA, AMT = 0,       PNA  = PNA_0 + TIME / 30.44,       PAGE = GA / 4.35 + PNA,       WT   = who_weight(pmax(0, PNA), wt_z)     )    d_events <- bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>     arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) |>     select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV, WT, PAGE,            RACE_BLACK_OTH, RACE_ASIAN_AMIND_MULTI, SEASON2, ADA_POS, WT_0)    out <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = d_events) |>     as.data.frame()    # NCA by individual   nca <- out |>     group_by(id) |>     summarise(       AUC0_365 = {         t <- time; c <- Cc         sum(diff(t) * (c[-length(c)] + c[-1]) / 2) # trapezoid       },       Cmax     = max(Cc),       C_day151 = Cc[which.min(abs(time - 151))],       WT_base  = WT_0[1],       .groups  = "drop"     ) |>     mutate(ga_group = g$label) })  d_f6_nca <- bind_rows(sim_f6_list) |>   mutate(ga_group = factor(ga_group, levels = sapply(ga_groups, `[[`, "label"))) # 90% prediction intervals (5th-95th) by baseline weight bin wt_bins <- seq(1, 11, by = 0.5)  d_f6_long <- d_f6_nca |>   pivot_longer(c(AUC0_365, Cmax, C_day151), names_to = "metric", values_to = "value") |>   mutate(wt_bin = cut(WT_base, breaks = wt_bins, right = FALSE))  d_f6_pi <- d_f6_long |>   group_by(ga_group, metric, wt_bin) |>   summarise(     wt_mid = mean(WT_base),     Q05    = quantile(value, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50    = quantile(value, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95    = quantile(value, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   filter(!is.na(wt_bin))  metric_labels <- c(   AUC0_365 = "AUC[0-365]~(day%.%mg/mL)",   Cmax     = "C[max]~(mu*g/mL)",   C_day151 = "C[day~151]~(mu*g/mL)" ) # Adult reference values (3000 mg IV) adult_refs <- data.frame(   metric = c("AUC0_365", "Cmax"),   yref   = c(57.0, 1140) ) ec90_val <- 6.8  # preclinical EC90, μg/mL  ggplot(d_f6_pi, aes(x = wt_mid)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), fill = "#87ceeb", alpha = 0.6) +   geom_line(aes(y = Q50), linewidth = 0.8) +   # Individual posthoc predictions   geom_point(     data = d_f6_nca |>       pivot_longer(c(AUC0_365, Cmax, C_day151), names_to = "metric", values_to = "value"),     aes(x = WT_base, y = value),     colour = "grey50", size = 0.5, alpha = 0.4   ) +   # Adult reference lines (AUC and Cmax panels only)   geom_hline(     data = adult_refs,     aes(yintercept = yref),     linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 0.7   ) +   # EC90 line (Day-151 panel only)   geom_hline(     data = data.frame(metric = "C_day151", yref = ec90_val),     aes(yintercept = yref),     linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.7   ) +   facet_grid(     metric ~ ga_group,     scales = "free_y",     labeller = labeller(metric = as_labeller(metric_labels, label_parsed))   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1, 11, by = 2)) +   labs(     x       = "Baseline weight (kg)",     y       = NULL,     title   = "Figure 6 \u2014 Predicted exposure vs body weight by GA group",     caption = paste0(       "Blue band: 90% prediction interval (5th\u201395th percentile). ",       "Black line: median. Grey dots: individual predictions.\n",       "Dotted line: adult reference (3000 mg IV). ",       "Dashed line: preclinical EC\u2090 = 6.8 \u03bcg/mL (Day-151 panel).\n",       "White race, ADA negative, Season 1 assumed."     )   ) +   theme_bw() +   theme(     strip.text.x = element_text(size = 9),     strip.text.y = element_text(size = 8)   ) #> `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation. #> ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic? #> `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation. #> ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic? #> `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation. #> ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic? #> `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation. #> ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic? #> `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation. #> ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic? #> `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation. #> ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic? #> `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation. #> ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic? #> `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation. #> ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic? #> `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation. #> ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic?

title: Clegg_2024_nirsevimab
text: Model: 2-compartment first-order IM absorption; published parameters 70 kg adult reference, scaled allometrically infant weights. Virtual populations: Covariate distributions match published trial demographics (Table 1). Race set White/Native Hawaiian reference ADA negative simulated subjects isolate weight maturation effects. Weight trajectories: weight--age LMS growth standards (combined sex, 0–24 months), infant’s growth percentile (z-score) held constant. IIV: Simulated using published omega² values: CL (26% CV), V2 (43% CV), Ka (44% CV; high shrinkage 83%), correlation r = 0.785 CL V2. Q V3 IIV final model. Residual error: Proportional 21%. Figure 6 NCA: AUC₀₋₃₆₅ computed trapezoidal rule daily simulated concentrations; C_max Day-151 concentration derived densely sampled time course.

title: Clegg_2024_nirsevimab
text: Clegg L, Freshwater E, Leach , Villafana T, Wahlby Hamren U. Population Pharmacokinetics Nirsevimab Preterm Term Infants. J Clin Pharmacol. 2024;64(5):555-567. doi:10.1002/jcph.2401

what: Simulate trontinemab PK in plasma and brain
title: Trontinemab and gantenerumab PK in plasma and brain (Grimm 2023)
text: Replicate figures 2 3 publication single 10 mg/kg dose cynomolgus monkey.
code: dSimDose <-   data.frame(     ID = 1,     AMT = 10, # mg/kg     WT = 5, # cynomologus monkey body weight according to the paper     TIME = 0,     EVID = 1,     CMT = "central"   ) dSimObs <-   data.frame(     ID = 1,     AMT = 0,     WT = 5,     TIME = c(5/60, 1, 2, 4, 8, 12, seq(24, 336, by = 24)),     EVID = 0,     CMT = "central"   ) dSimPrep <- dplyr::bind_rows(dSimDose, dSimObs) Grimm2023Tront <- readModelDb("Grimm_2023_trontinemab") # Set BSV to zero for simulation to get a reproducible result dSimTront <- rxode2::rxSolve(Grimm2023Tront |> rxode2::zeroRe(), events = dSimPrep) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalfpla_cerebellum', 'etalfpla_hippocampus', 'etalfpla_striatum', 'etalfpla_cortex', 'etalfpla_choroid_plexus' dSimTront$Analyte <- "Trontinemab"

what: Simulate gantenerumab PK in plasma and brain
title: Trontinemab and gantenerumab PK in plasma and brain (Grimm 2023)
text: Replicate figures 2 3 publication single 20 mg/kg dose cynomolgus monkey.
code: dSimDose <-   data.frame(     ID = 1,     AMT = 20, # mg/kg     WT = 5, # cynomologus monkey body weight according to the paper     TIME = 0,     EVID = 1,     CMT = "central"   ) dSimObs <-   data.frame(     ID = 1,     AMT = 0,     WT = 5,     TIME = c(5/60, 1, 2, 4, 8, 12, seq(24, 336, by = 24)),     EVID = 0,     CMT = "central"   ) dSimPrep <- dplyr::bind_rows(dSimDose, dSimObs) Grimm2023Gant <- readModelDb("Grimm_2023_gantenerumab") # Set BSV to zero for simulation to get a reproducible result dSimGant <- rxode2::rxSolve(Grimm2023Gant |> rxode2::zeroRe(), events = dSimPrep) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalfpla_cerebellum', 'etalfpla_hippocampus', 'etalfpla_striatum', 'etalfpla_cortex', 'etalfpla_choroid_plexus' dSimGant$Analyte <- "Gantenerumab"

title: Trontinemab and gantenerumab PK in plasma and brain (Grimm 2023)
text: Replicate figure 2 paper.
code: dSim <- bind_rows(dSimTront, dSimGant) dSim$Analyte <- factor(dSim$Analyte, levels = c("Trontinemab", "Gantenerumab"))  ggplot(dSim, aes(x = time, y = sim)) +   geom_line() +   labs(     x = "Time (h)",     y = "Concentration (ng/mL)"   ) +   scale_y_log10() +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 336, by = 48)) +   coord_cartesian(ylim = c(1e3, NA)) +   facet_grid(~Analyte)

title: Trontinemab and gantenerumab PK in plasma and brain (Grimm 2023)
text: Replicate figure 3 paper.
code: d_plot_brain <-   dSim |>   select(time, Analyte, starts_with("C", ignore.case = FALSE)) |>   select(-starts_with("Cbrain"), -Cc) |>   tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = -c("time", "Analyte"), names_to = "ASPEC", values_to = "AVAL") |>   mutate(     ASPEC =       factor(         gsub(x = ASPEC, pattern = "^C", replacement = ""),         levels = c("hippocampus", "cerebellum", "choroid_plexus", "cortex", "striatum", "csf"),         labels = c("Hippocampus", "Cerebellum", "Choroid Plexus", "Cortex", "Striatum", "Cerebrospinal Fluid")       )   )  ggplot(d_plot_brain, aes(x = time, y = AVAL, colour = Analyte)) +   geom_line() +   labs(     x = "Time (h)",     y = "Concentration (ng/g)"   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(0, 72, 168, 240, 336)) +   facet_wrap(~ASPEC)

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
text: Replicate Figure 2 Hu et al. (2026): prediction-corrected visual predictive check (VPC) showing clesrovimab serum concentrations 150 days single 105 mg intramuscular dose, presented overall population panel panel stratified baseline body weight category. original study data (5,850 PK samples 2,942 infants across three trials) publicly available, vignette simulates virtual infant population using covariate distributions approximate study demographics (median age 3.02 months, median weight 5.4 kg, preterm full-term infants). Body weight trajectories follow-generated using weight--age growth standards (combined sex, 0–10 months).

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
text: weight--age LMS parameters combined sex, 0–10 months (Multicentre Growth Reference Study Group, 2006). LMS formula : weight = M × (1 + L × S × z)^(1/L)
code: who_lms <- data.frame(   age_mo = 0:10,   L = c(0.3487, 0.2297, 0.1970, 0.1738, 0.1553, 0.1395,         0.1257, 0.1125, 0.0998, 0.0875, 0.0756),   M = c(3.3464, 4.4709, 5.5675, 6.3762, 7.0023, 7.5105,         7.9340, 8.2970, 8.6151, 8.9014, 9.1649),   S = c(0.14602, 0.13395, 0.12385, 0.11876, 0.11535, 0.11254,         0.11056, 0.10947, 0.10868, 0.10814, 0.10765) )  # Returns weight (kg) for a given age (months) and individual z-score # using linear interpolation of LMS parameters between whole-month values who_weight <- function(age_mo, z) {   age_mo <- pmax(0, pmin(age_mo, 10))   L <- approx(who_lms$age_mo, who_lms$L, xout = age_mo)$y   M <- approx(who_lms$age_mo, who_lms$M, xout = age_mo)$y   S <- approx(who_lms$age_mo, who_lms$S, xout = age_mo)$y   M * (1 + L * S * z)^(1 / L) }

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
text: Sample N = 500 virtual infants covariate distributions approximating study population.
code: set.seed(1654) # clesrovimab MK-1654 n_subj <- 500  # Gestational age: 85% full-term (37-42 wk), 15% preterm (32-37 wk) is_preterm <- runif(n_subj) < 0.15 GA <- ifelse(is_preterm, runif(n_subj, 32, 37), runif(n_subj, 37, 42))  # Postnatal age at dosing (months); study enrolled infants <= 3 months PNA_0 <- runif(n_subj, 0, 3)  # Individual weight z-score (fixed percentile across follow-up) # Truncated to ±2 SD to avoid extreme weights wt_z <- pmax(-2, pmin(2, rnorm(n_subj, 0, 1)))  # Race: approximate study demographics race_draw <- runif(n_subj) RACE_ASIAN <- as.integer(race_draw < 0.15) RACE_BLACK <- as.integer(race_draw >= 0.15 & race_draw < 0.35) RACE_MULTI <- as.integer(race_draw >= 0.35 & race_draw < 0.40) # White/Other: race_draw >= 0.40 (reference; all indicators = 0)  pop <- data.frame(   ID         = seq_len(n_subj),   GA         = GA,   PNA_0      = PNA_0,   wt_z       = wt_z,   RACE_ASIAN = RACE_ASIAN,   RACE_BLACK = RACE_BLACK,   RACE_MULTI = RACE_MULTI )  # Baseline weight for stratification pop$WT_base <- who_weight(pop$PNA_0, pop$wt_z) pop$wt_group <- cut(   pop$WT_base,   breaks = c(0, 4, 6, Inf),   labels = c("< 4 kg", "4\u20136 kg", "\u2265 6 kg"),   right  = FALSE )

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
text: Observation times every 7 days 0 150 days, plus dose event time 0.
code: obs_times_day <- seq(0, 150, by = 7)  # Dose records (one per subject at time 0) d_dose <- pop |>   mutate(     TIME = 0,     AMT  = 105,    # mg, single IM dose     EVID = 1,     CMT  = "depot",     DV   = NA   )  # Observation records with time-varying WT and PNA d_obs <- pop[rep(seq_len(n_subj), each = length(obs_times_day)), ] |>   mutate(     TIME = rep(obs_times_day, times = n_subj),     AMT  = 0,     EVID = 0,     CMT  = "central",     DV   = NA,     PNA  = PNA_0 + TIME / 30.44,      # postnatal age in months     WT   = who_weight(PNA, wt_z)       # time-varying weight from WHO curves   )  # Dose records also need WT and PNA at time 0 d_dose <- d_dose |>   mutate(     PNA = PNA_0,     WT  = who_weight(PNA_0, wt_z)   )  d_sim <- bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) |>   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) |>   select(ID, TIME, AMT, EVID, CMT, DV, WT, PNA, GA, RACE_ASIAN, RACE_BLACK, RACE_MULTI,          wt_group, wt_z)

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Hu_2026_clesrovimab")  # Simulate: rxSolve uses the N=500 subjects' IIV from the model ini() block. # Setting seed for reproducibility. set.seed(2026) sim_out <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = d_sim) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
code: # Attach baseline weight group to simulation output wt_grp_map <- d_sim |>   filter(EVID == 0, TIME == 0) |>   select(ID, wt_group) |>   distinct()  sim_plot <- sim_out |>   as.data.frame() |>   filter(time > 0) |>                 # exclude pre-dose time point   left_join(wt_grp_map, by = c("id" = "ID"))  # Overall population quantiles d_overall <- sim_plot |>   group_by(time) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   mutate(panel = "Overall")  # Weight-stratified quantiles d_strat <- sim_plot |>   group_by(time, wt_group) |>   summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   rename(panel = wt_group)

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
code: ggplot(d_overall, aes(x = time, y = Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_y_log10(     labels = scales::label_number(drop0trailing = TRUE)   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 150, by = 30)) +   labs(     x    = "Time after dose (days)",     y    = "Clesrovimab concentration (\u03bcg/mL)",     title = "Overall population\nMedian and 90% prediction interval"   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Figure 2 — Stratified by baseline body weight
title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
code: ggplot(d_strat, aes(x = time, y = Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.25) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   facet_wrap(~panel, nrow = 1) +   scale_y_log10(     labels = scales::label_number(drop0trailing = TRUE)   ) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 150, by = 60)) +   labs(     x    = "Time after dose (days)",     y    = "Clesrovimab concentration (\u03bcg/mL)",     title = "By baseline body weight category\nMedian and 90% prediction interval"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
text: Virtual population: 500 infants GA, postnatal age, weight percentile, race sampled approximate three-study population (MK-1654-002, CLEVER, SMART). Time-varying weight: Individual weight trajectories generated using weight--age growth standards (LMS method), subject assigned fixed z-score (growth percentile) enrollment. IIV: Simulated using published omega^2 values Ka (23.5% CV), CL/F (14.4% CV), Vc/F (8.12% CV; note high shrinkage 71.9% original analysis). Q/F Vp/F IIV final model. Residual error: Combined proportional (14.3%) additive (0.231 µg/mL). prediction intervals reflect inter-individual variability residual error final population PK model.

title: Hu_2026_clesrovimab
text: Hu Z, Hellmann F, Zang X, et al. Population Pharmacokinetics Clesrovimab Preterm Full-Term Infants. Clin Pharmacol Ther. 2026;119(4):1036-1046. doi:10.1002/cpt.70199

title: Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab
text: Citation: Kovalenko P, Davis JD, Li M, et al. Base Covariate Population Pharmacokinetic Analyses Dupilumab Using Phase 3 Data. Clinical Pharmacology Drug Development. 2020;9(6):756-767. doi:10.1002/cpdd.780 Description: Dupilumab PK model (Kovalenko 2020) Article: Clin Pharmacol Drug Dev. 2020;9(6):756-767 (open access via PMC7496533)

title: Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab
text: Kovalenko 2020 pooled 16 clinical studies (N = 2115 participants; 202 healthy volunteers 1913 patients moderate--severe atopic dermatitis (AD)). , 2041 participants active treatment contributed 18,243 20,809 samples population PK analysis. Source studies include Phase 3 AD trials R668-AD-1334 (LIBERTY AD SOLO 1), R668-AD-1416 (LIBERTY AD SOLO 2), R668-AD-1224 (LIBERTY AD CHRONOS). Phase 1 studies R668--0907, TDU12265, PKM14161, R668-AD-1117 used fit “Model 1” - rich-data model file implements. paper’s main text tabulate detailed baseline demographics (age, sex, weight median/range, race breakdown) pooled cohort; appear Supplementary Table S1. approved adult AD maintenance regimen 600 mg SC loading dose day 0 followed 300 mg SC every 2 weeks (Q2W); Kovalenko 2016 precursor publication used 75 kg reference body weight allometric effect central volume, 2020 paper reuses equation without re-stating reference weight. information available programmatically via readModelDb("Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab")$population.

title: Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab
text: Every structural parameter, covariate effect, IIV element, residual-error term taken Kovalenko 2020 Table 1 (Model 1 column) Supplementary Table S2 (IIV SDs residual errors). paper--implementation cross-walk: paper’s Methods section explicitly defines omega “omega (omega, standard deviation [SD] -subject variability)” sigma “sigma (sigma, SD measurement error)”. nlmixr2’s etalxxx ~ value syntax stores variance (omega^2), Supp. Table S2 SDs squared ini(). Model 1 shrinkage SD reported paper (10.4% / 25.2% / 22.9% / 23.2% / 54.9% Vc / ke / Vm / ka / MTT) consistent IIV--SD accounting.

title: Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab
text: Kovalenko 2020 parameterizes linear elimination first-order rate ke * central (CL * Cc) distribution direct rate constants kcp kpc = kcp / Mpc (Q Vp). model file preserves parameterization, derived quantities : Typical clearance: CL = ke * Vc = 0.0534 * 2.48 = 0.132 L/day (matches Table 1 “CL (L/d)”). Typical peripheral rate kpc = 0.213 / 0.686 = 0.310 1/day. etalmtt eta applied MTT = exp(lmtt + etalmtt) (.e. log-normal MTT). Supplementary Table S2 paper reports random effect additively MTT; log-normal implementation chosen avoids negative MTT draws simulation deviation published random-effect structure.

title: Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab
text: Detailed observed demographics pooled cohort publicly reproduced paper’s main text. virtual cohort targets labelled adult AD population drawing body weight truncated normal centered Kovalenko 2016 reference weight 75 kg (SD ~18 kg, limits 40-165 kg), matches typical Phase 3 AD trial demographics reported Simpson 2016 SOLO / Blauvelt 2017 CHRONOS publications.
code: set.seed(20260418) n_subj <- 400  cohort <- tibble::tibble(   id = seq_len(n_subj),   WT = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 75, sd = 18), 40), 165) )  # Labelled AD regimen: 600 mg SC loading dose on day 0, 300 mg SC Q2W # for 12 additional doses -> study window 0-182 days. By dose 10+ the # profile is close to steady state (typical half-life ~2-3 weeks). load_dose  <- 600 maint_dose <- 300 tau        <- 14 n_maint    <- 12 dose_days  <- c(0, seq(tau, tau * n_maint, by = tau)) amt_vec    <- c(load_dose, rep(maint_dose, n_maint))  ev_dose <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(time = dose_days) |>   dplyr::arrange(id, time) |>   dplyr::group_by(id) |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = amt_vec, cmt = "depot", evid = 1L) |>   dplyr::ungroup()  obs_days <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, tau * (n_maint + 1), by = 1),   dose_days + 0.25,   dose_days + 1,   dose_days + 3 )))  ev_obs <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(time = obs_days) |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L)  events <- dplyr::bind_rows(ev_dose, ev_obs) |>   dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, cmt, evid, WT)

title: Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab
code: mod <- rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab")) #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()' sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events, keep = "WT")

title: Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab
text: Kovalenko 2020 Figure 5 illustrates simulated median concentration-time profiles functional dupilumab several SC maintenance regimens (qw, q2w, q4w, q8w). figure reproduces labelled adult AD regimen (600 mg SC loading + 300 mg SC Q2W) 5th/50th/95th percentile bands across virtual cohort, spanning ~13 dosing cycles.
code: vpc <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time > 0) |>   dplyr::group_by(time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(vpc, aes(time, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.2, fill = "#4682b4") +   geom_line(colour = "#4682b4", linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_vline(xintercept = dose_days, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey70") +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, NA)) +   labs(     x = "Time (days)",     y = "Dupilumab Cc (mg/L)",     title = "Simulated 5-50-95 percentile profile; 600 mg SC load then 300 mg SC Q2W",     caption = "Virtual AD cohort (N = 400); WT ~N(75, 18) kg, truncated 40-165 kg."   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab
text: Zoomed-view final Q2W cycle (days 168-182) isolate steady-state peak, trough, AUC_tau used NCA .
code: ss_start <- tau * n_maint        # day 168 (time of dose 13) ss_end   <- ss_start + tau       # day 182  ss_summary <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end, !is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::group_by(time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(ss_summary, aes(time - ss_start, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.2, fill = "#b4464b") +   geom_line(colour = "#b4464b", linewidth = 0.8) +   labs(     x = "Time after final dose (days)",     y = "Dupilumab Cc (mg/L)",     title = "Steady-state Q2W cycle (dose 13 of 13)",     caption = "Median and 90% prediction interval over one 14-day dosing interval."   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab
text: Non-compartmental analysis steady-state Q2W interval (days 168-182). Compute Cmax, Cmin (Ctrough end tau), AUC_tau, average concentration per simulated subject.
code: nca_conc <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end, !is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::mutate(time_nom = time - ss_start,                 treatment = "300mg_Q2W_SS") |>   dplyr::select(id, time = time_nom, Cc, treatment)  nca_dose <- cohort |>   dplyr::mutate(time = 0, amt = maint_dose, treatment = "300mg_Q2W_SS") |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(nca_conc, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(nca_dose, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start  = 0,   end    = tau,   cmax   = TRUE,   cmin   = TRUE,   auclast = TRUE,   cav    = TRUE )  nca_res <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals)) #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■                     40% |  ETA:  2s summary(nca_res) #>  start end    treatment   N     auclast        cmax        cmin         cav #>      0  14 300mg_Q2W_SS 400 1190 [43.9] 95.0 [41.9] 70.4 [49.2] 85.1 [43.9] #>  #> Caption: auclast, cmax, cmin, cav: geometric mean and geometric coefficient of variation; N: number of subjects

what: Comparison against published typical steady-state exposure
title: Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab
text: Kovalenko 2020 tabulate point estimates steady-state Cmax, Ctrough, AUC_tau main text; Figure 5 shows simulated medians qualitatively. Dupilumab FDA labelling follow-PK publications report typical steady-state trough concentrations ~70-80 mg/L 75-kg adult 600-mg-load + 300-mg-Q2W SC regimen. typical-value (“population typical”) prediction IIV zeroed provides direct self-consistency check: Typical-subject steady-state exposure (WT = 75 kg; IIV zeroed).
code: mod_typical <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe()  ev_typical <- events |>   dplyr::filter(id == 1L) |>   dplyr::mutate(WT = 75)  sim_typical <- rxode2::rxSolve(   mod_typical, events = ev_typical, keep = "WT" ) |>   as.data.frame() #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalvc', 'etalke', 'etalka', 'etalvm', 'etalmtt'  ss_typical <- sim_typical |>   dplyr::filter(time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end, !is.na(Cc))  typical_summary <- tibble::tibble(   metric = c("Cmax (mg/L)", "Cmin/Ctrough (mg/L)",              "Cavg (mg/L)", "AUC_tau (day*mg/L)"),   typical_value = c(     max(ss_typical$Cc),     min(ss_typical$Cc),     mean(ss_typical$Cc),     sum(diff(ss_typical$time) *           (ss_typical$Cc[-length(ss_typical$Cc)] +              ss_typical$Cc[-1]) / 2)   ) ) knitr::kable(typical_summary, digits = 2,              caption = "Typical-subject steady-state exposure (WT = 75 kg; IIV zeroed).")

title: Kovalenko_2020_dupilumab
text: Kovalenko 2020 Model 1 uses ke/kcp/kpc parameterization rather CL/Q/Vp. model file preserves original parameterization verbatim. Typical values can cross-checked Table 1’s derived CL = ke * Vc = 0.132 L/day. Km F reported fixed Table 1 Model 1, values carried earlier Kovalenko 2016 model; CcaddSd likewise fixed 0.03 mg/L. etalmtt eta applied log-normal MTT (MTT = exp(lmtt + etalmtt)), whereas Supplementary Table S2 implements random effect additively MTT. log-normal form prevents non-physical negative MTT draws simulation. etas match paper’s random-effect structure directly. IIV (omega) reported Kovalenko 2020 standard deviation, per Methods definition. Values ini() squared yield variance nlmixr2 stores ~ operator. earlier version file stored SDs directly RHS (.e. variances), understate IIV magnitude mAb-relevant scale; current file corrected form. reference body weight (75 kg) inherited Kovalenko 2016 (doi:10.1002/psp4.12136); Kovalenko 2020 restate reference weight. See file header comment details. Demographic details (age, sex, race) used Model 1 implementation (WT enters covariate model) re-simulated virtual cohort. Kovalenko 2020 publish numerical Cmax / Cmin / AUC_tau steady state, PKNCA summary self-consistency check implemented model visual appearance Figure 5 rather back--paper numerical comparison. unit conversion required dosing amount (mg) concentration unit (mg/L) mg/L natural unit central/vc .

title: Kyhl_2016_nalmefene
text: Replicate figures 5 publication single 20 mg dose fed state. Assumed mean age, lean body mass, tablet, LC-MS/MS assay used. paper indicates simulations used uniform age distribution 18-80 years LBM ~N(56,72). Since limits provided LBM simulation settings, simulation settings used.
code: dSimDose <-   data.frame(     ID = 1,     AMT = 20, # mg/kg     TIME = 0,     EVID = 1,     CMT = "depot"   ) dSimObs <-   data.frame(     ID = 1,     AMT = 0,     WT = 5,     TIME = seq(0, 24, by = 0.1),     EVID = 0,     CMT = "central"   ) dSimPrep <-   dplyr::bind_rows(dSimDose, dSimObs) |>   dplyr::mutate(     LBM = 56.28,     AGE = 28,     RIA_ASSAY = 0,     FED = 1,     TABLET = 1   ) Kyhl2016Nalmefene <- readModelDb("Kyhl_2016_nalmefene") # Set BSV to zero for simulation to get a reproducible result dSimNalmefene <- rxode2::rxSolve(Kyhl2016Nalmefene, events = dSimPrep, nStud = 500) dSimNalmefene$Analyte <- "Nalmefene"

title: Kyhl_2016_nalmefene
text: Replicate figure 5 paper. Assuming “confidence bounds” actually 95% prediction intervals.
code: dSimNalmefenePlot <-   dSimNalmefene |>   group_by(time) |>   summarize(     Q025_pk = quantile(sim, probs = 0.025),     Q50_pk = quantile(sim, probs = 0.5),     Q975_pk = quantile(sim, probs = 0.975),     Q025_occ = quantile(e_mu_opioid, probs = 0.025),     Q50_occ = quantile(e_mu_opioid, probs = 0.5),     Q975_occ = quantile(e_mu_opioid, probs = 0.975)   )  ggplot(dSimNalmefenePlot, aes(x = time, y = Q50_pk, ymin = Q025_pk, ymax = Q975_pk)) +   geom_line() +   labs(     x = "Time (h)",     y = "Estimated plasma concentration (ng mL^-1)"   ) +   geom_ribbon(fill = "gray") +   geom_line() +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 24, by = 5)) ggplot(dSimNalmefenePlot, aes(x = time, y = Q50_occ, ymin = Q025_occ, ymax = Q975_occ)) +   geom_line() +   labs(     x = "Time (h)",     y = "Occupancy (%)"   ) +   geom_ribbon(fill = "gray") +   geom_line() +   geom_hline(yintercept = c(60, 90)) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 24, by = 5))

title: Ogasawara_2020_durvalumab
text: Simulate durvalumab concentration-time profiles using final population PK model Ogasawara et al. (2020) patients hematologic malignancies (MDS/AML multiple myeloma, N = 267). Durvalumab human IgG1 kappa anti-PD-L1 checkpoint inhibitor. model 2-compartment IV model linear elimination extensive covariate effects (11 covariate-parameter relationships). Anti-drug antibodies (ADA) examined covariate analysis. Note: Time units hours (matching original publication). Source: Table 3 Ogasawara et al. (2020) Clin Pharmacokinet. 59(2):217-227. doi:10.1007/s40262-019-00804-x. Parameters equations verified PMC7007418 (Table 3 footnotes b c).

title: Ogasawara_2020_durvalumab
code: set.seed(2020) n_subj <- 500  pop <- data.frame(   ID     = seq_len(n_subj),   WT     = rlnorm(n_subj, log(74.7), 0.25),  # Median 74.7 kg (Table 2)   ALB    = rnorm(n_subj, 40, 5),              # Median 40 g/L (Table 2)   IGG    = rlnorm(n_subj, log(7.6), 0.35),   # Median 7.6 g/L (Table 2)   SPDL1  = rlnorm(n_subj, log(173.8), 0.8),  # Median 173.8 pg/mL (Table 2)   LDH    = rlnorm(n_subj, log(216), 0.4),    # Median 216 U/L (Table 2)   SEX    = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.352),          # 35.2% female (Table 2)   MDSAML = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.37),           # 37% MDS/AML (Table 2)   MM     = 0 ) pop$ALB <- pmax(20, pmin(pop$ALB, 55)) # MM is complement of MDSAML (simplified: NHL excluded) pop$MM <- ifelse(pop$MDSAML == 0, rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.73), 0)

title: Ogasawara_2020_durvalumab
text: Simulate durvalumab 1500 mg IV every 4 weeks (q4w) 24 weeks. Infusion 1 hour.
code: dose_weeks <- seq(0, 20, by = 4) dose_times_h <- dose_weeks * 7 * 24  # weeks to hours  obs_times_h <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, 48, by = 4),                            # Dense first 2 days   seq(48, max(dose_times_h) + 28 * 24, by = 24)  # Daily to end )))  d_dose <- pop %>%   crossing(TIME = dose_times_h) %>%   mutate(AMT = 1500, EVID = 1, CMT = 1, DUR = 1, DV = NA_real_)  d_obs <- pop %>%   crossing(TIME = obs_times_h) %>%   mutate(AMT = NA_real_, EVID = 0, CMT = 1, DUR = NA_real_, DV = NA_real_)  d_sim <- bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) %>%   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) %>%   as.data.frame()

title: Ogasawara_2020_durvalumab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Ogasawara_2020_durvalumab") sim <- rxSolve(mod, d_sim, returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

title: Ogasawara_2020_durvalumab
code: sim_summary <- sim %>%   filter(time > 0) %>%   group_by(time) %>%   summarise(     median = median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     lo     = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     hi     = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(sim_summary, aes(x = time / (24 * 7))) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.2, fill = "purple4") +   geom_line(aes(y = median), color = "purple4", linewidth = 1) +   labs(     x = "Time (weeks)",     y = "Durvalumab concentration (mg/L)",     title = "Simulated durvalumab PK (1500 mg IV q4w)",     subtitle = "Median and 90% PI (N = 500 virtual hematologic malignancy patients)",     caption = "Model: Ogasawara et al. (2020) Clin Pharmacokinet"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Ogasawara_2020_durvalumab
code: # The rxSolve output carries covariates through, so MDSAML is already in sim sim_df_disease <- as.data.frame(sim) sim_df_disease <- sim_df_disease[sim_df_disease$time > 0, ] sim_df_disease$Disease <- ifelse(sim_df_disease$MDSAML == 1, "MDS/AML", "Other")  sim_disease_summary <- sim_df_disease %>%   group_by(time, Disease) %>%   summarise(median = median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")  ggplot(sim_disease_summary, aes(x = time / (24 * 7), y = median, color = Disease)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   scale_color_manual(values = c("MDS/AML" = "firebrick", "Other" = "navy")) +   labs(     x = "Time (weeks)",     y = "Durvalumab concentration (mg/L)",     title = "Durvalumab median concentrations by disease type",     subtitle = "MDS/AML patients have 26% higher CL and lower exposures",     caption = "Model: Ogasawara et al. (2020)"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Ogasawara_2020_durvalumab
text: NCA summary (3rd dosing interval, weeks 8-12)
code: # Use 3rd dosing interval (weeks 8-12 = hours 1344-2016) sim_df <- as.data.frame(sim) # Build unique subject key from sim.id and id if (all(c("sim.id", "id") %in% names(sim_df))) {   sim_df$subject <- paste(sim_df$sim.id, sim_df$id, sep = "_") } else if ("id" %in% names(sim_df)) {   sim_df$subject <- sim_df$id } else {   sim_df$subject <- sim_df$sim.id } nca_data <- data.frame(   subject  = sim_df$subject,   time_rel = (sim_df$time - 1344) / 24,  # Convert to days relative to dose 3   Cc       = sim_df$Cc ) nca_data <- nca_data[nca_data$time_rel >= 0 & nca_data$time_rel <= 28 & nca_data$Cc > 0, ]  conc_obj <- PKNCAconc(nca_data, Cc ~ time_rel | subject) dose_obj <- PKNCAdose(   data.frame(subject = unique(nca_data$subject), time_rel = 0, AMT = 1500),   AMT ~ time_rel | subject ) data_obj <- PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj,                        intervals = data.frame(start = 0, end = 28,                                               cmax = TRUE, tmax = TRUE,                                               auclast = TRUE, half.life = TRUE)) nca_results <- pk.nca(data_obj) #>  ■■■■■■■                           21% |  ETA: 10s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■                   45% |  ETA:  7s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■            69% |  ETA:  4s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■     93% |  ETA:  1s nca_summary <- summary(nca_results) knitr::kable(nca_summary, digits = 2,              caption = "NCA summary (3rd dosing interval, weeks 8-12)")

title: Ogasawara_2020_durvalumab
text: Model: 2-compartment IV linear elimination. TMDD. 11 covariate effects including soluble PD-L1 (drug’s target) CL. Log-additive residual error (concentrations log-transformed prior modeling). Time hours — unlike mAb models use days. ADA examined covariate analysis. Reference values Table 2 medians: ALB 40 g/L, IgG 7.61 g/L, sPD-L1 173.8 pg/mL, LDH 216 U/L, WT 74.7 kg. Albumin, IgG, sPD-L1, LDH time-varying covariates model. MDS/AML effect: 26% higher CL (clinically meaningful).

title: PK_2cmt_mAb_Davda_2014
text: Citation: Davda JP, Dodds MG, Gibbs MA, Wisdom W, Gibbs JP. model-based meta-analysis monoclonal antibody pharmacokinetics guide optimal first--human study design. MAbs. 2014;6(4):1094-1102. doi:10.4161/mabs.29095 Description: Two compartment PK model linear clearance average monoclonal antibodies (Davda 2014) Article: MAbs 2014;6(4):1094-1102 Open access: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4171012/

title: PK_2cmt_mAb_Davda_2014
text: Davda 2014 model-based meta-analysis first--human (FIH) population PK data four therapeutic monoclonal antibodies (labeled mAb , b, c, d). mAb , b, c humanized/human IgG2 antibodies; mAb d human IgG1. four target soluble ligands. pooled dataset comprised 171 healthy adult volunteers (2,716 serum concentrations: 1,153 IV dosing 1,563 SC dosing). IV dose range 1–700 mg SC dose range 2.1–700 mg. Per-study demographic breakdowns (age, sex, weight bands, race) tabulated published paper; allometric reference weight 70 kg. resulting consensus parameters (Table 3) intended guide FIH dose selection new therapeutic mAbs soluble targets similar properties. information available programmatically via readModelDb("PK_2cmt_mAb_Davda_2014")$population.

title: PK_2cmt_mAb_Davda_2014
text: Per-parameter origin recorded -file comment next ini() entry inst/modeldb/pharmacokinetics/PK_2cmt_mAb_Davda_2014.R. table collects review. Davda 2014 uses convention reported “%CV” omega × 100 omega standard deviation log-normally distributed random effect. diagonal OMEGA therefore simply (CV/100)^2. encoding used ini() block .

title: PK_2cmt_mAb_Davda_2014
text: Individual observed data public. cohort virtual population healthy adults body weight sampled truncated normal centered 70 kg (SD 12 kg, truncated 45-120 kg), simulated two regimens: IV 2 mg/kg instantaneous bolus central compartment. SC 2 mg/kg depot compartment (bioavailability model). follow one subject 84 days capture terminal phase average mAb (apparent half-life ~20 days).
code: set.seed(20260418) n_subj <- 200  wt_draws <- pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 70, sd = 12), 45), 120)  cohort <- tibble(   id = seq_len(n_subj),   WT = wt_draws )  obs_times <- c(   seq(0, 1, by = 1/24),        # first 24 h hourly   seq(1.25, 7, by = 0.25),     # first week quarter-day   seq(7.5, 28, by = 0.5),      # out to day 28 twice-daily   seq(29, 84, by = 1)          # to day 84 daily )  make_events <- function(cohort, route = c("IV", "SC")) {   route <- match.arg(route)   cmt_dose <- if (route == "IV") "central" else "depot"   doses <- cohort |>     dplyr::mutate(       time = 0,       amt = 2 * WT,             # 2 mg/kg       cmt = cmt_dose,       evid = 1L     ) |>     dplyr::select(id, time, amt, cmt, evid, WT)   obs <- cohort |>     tidyr::crossing(time = obs_times) |>     dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L) |>     dplyr::select(id, time, amt, cmt, evid, WT)   dplyr::bind_rows(doses, obs) |>     dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) |>     dplyr::mutate(treatment = route) }  events_iv <- make_events(cohort, "IV") events_sc <- make_events(cohort, "SC")

title: PK_2cmt_mAb_Davda_2014
text: deterministic typical-subject (70 kg, -subject variability) comparison IV vs SC, zero random effects:
code: mod <- rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("PK_2cmt_mAb_Davda_2014"))  sim_iv <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events_iv, keep = c("WT", "treatment")) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalfdepot' sim_sc <- rxode2::rxSolve(mod, events = events_sc, keep = c("WT", "treatment")) #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalfdepot'  sim <- dplyr::bind_rows(   as_tibble(sim_iv),   as_tibble(sim_sc) ) mod_typical <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe()  typical_cohort <- tibble(id = 1:2, WT = 70,                          treatment = c("IV", "SC"))  ev_typical <- dplyr::bind_rows(   tibble(id = 1, time = 0, amt = 140, cmt = "central", evid = 1L, WT = 70,          treatment = "IV"),   tibble(id = 2, time = 0, amt = 140, cmt = "depot",   evid = 1L, WT = 70,          treatment = "SC") ) |>   dplyr::bind_rows(     typical_cohort |>       tidyr::crossing(time = obs_times) |>       dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L)   ) |>   dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid))  sim_typical <- rxode2::rxSolve(   mod_typical, events = ev_typical, keep = c("WT", "treatment") ) |> as_tibble() #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalfdepot', 'etalka', 'etalcl', 'etalvc', 'etalvp', 'etalq' #> Warning: multi-subject simulation without without 'omega'

what: Figure 1 / 3 — typical IV vs SC profiles
title: PK_2cmt_mAb_Davda_2014
text: Davda 2014 Figure 1 shows representative concentration-time profiles IV SC administration included mAbs, Figure 3 shows VPC final model. typical-subject analogue: 70 kg adult receiving 2 mg/kg (140 mg) IV SC dose.
code: sim_typical |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), Cc > 0) |>   ggplot(aes(time, Cc, colour = treatment)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(x = "Time (days)", y = "Serum concentration (mg/L)",        colour = "Route",        title = "Typical mAb PK: 2 mg/kg IV vs SC (70 kg subject)",        caption = "Davda 2014 consensus parameters; typical values (no IIV). Compare to Figure 1 of Davda 2014.") +   theme_minimal() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: PK_2cmt_mAb_Davda_2014
text: Virtual cohort percentiles IIV IV SC regimens. Analogous Davda 2014 Figure 3 (VPC), though underlying individuals differ paper’s observed dataset.
code: sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), Cc > 0) |>   dplyr::group_by(time, treatment) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   ggplot(aes(time, Q50, fill = treatment, colour = treatment)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.2, colour = NA) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_y_log10() +   facet_wrap(~treatment) +   labs(x = "Time (days)", y = "Serum concentration (mg/L)",        title = "Simulated 5-50-95 percentiles",        caption = "2 mg/kg; virtual cohort (N = 200). Compare to Figure 3 of Davda 2014 (VPC).") +   theme_minimal() +   theme(legend.position = "none")

title: PK_2cmt_mAb_Davda_2014
text: Cmax, Tmax, AUC0-inf, terminal half-life computed PKNCA, stratified route (treatment grouping variable).
code: sim_nca <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), Cc > 0) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, Cc, treatment)  # IDs must be unique across treatment levels — namespace them sim_nca <- sim_nca |>   dplyr::mutate(id = paste(treatment, id, sep = "_"))  dose_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(events_iv, events_sc) |>   dplyr::filter(evid == 1) |>   dplyr::mutate(id = paste(treatment, id, sep = "_")) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(sim_nca, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(dose_df, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start      = 0,   end        = Inf,   cmax       = TRUE,   tmax       = TRUE,   aucinf.obs = TRUE,   half.life  = TRUE )  nca_res <- PKNCA::pk.nca(   PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals) ) #>  ■■■■                              10% |  ETA: 33s #>  ■■■■■■                            18% |  ETA: 30s #>  ■■■■■■■■■                         26% |  ETA: 27s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■                       34% |  ETA: 24s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■                    43% |  ETA: 20s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■                 52% |  ETA: 17s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■              63% |  ETA: 12s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■           75% |  ETA:  8s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■       87% |  ETA:  4s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■   99% |  ETA:  0s  summary(nca_res) #>  start end treatment   N        cmax                 tmax   half.life #>      0 Inf        IV 200 28.5 [35.4] 0.000 [0.000, 0.000] 24.1 [6.44] #>      0 Inf        SC 200 17.7 [33.0]    6.25 [1.25, 27.0] 25.0 [6.66] #>  aucinf.obs #>  506 [34.4] #>          NC #>  #> Caption: cmax, aucinf.obs: geometric mean and geometric coefficient of variation; tmax: median and range; half.life: arithmetic mean and standard deviation; N: number of subjects

title: PK_2cmt_mAb_Davda_2014
text: Davda 2014 population-PK meta-analysis report standalone NCA table Cmax / Tmax / AUC route. Instead, paper characterises mAb class via population parameter estimates Table 3. simulated NCA output provided readers can inspect emergent exposure properties consensus model. typical-subject sanity check 70 kg, 2 mg/kg IV / SC: Typical-subject (70 kg, 2 mg/kg) NCA Davda 2014 consensus model. paper publish NCA comparison; values shown reader inspection. Qualitative expectations Davda 2014 consensus parameters 70 kg, 2 mg/kg: IV Cmax t=0 ~ dose / Vc = 140 / 3.61 ≈ 39 mg/L. SC Cmax lower IV delayed slow first-order SC absorption (Ka = 0.282/day → absorption half-life ~2.5 days). Apparent terminal half-life average mAb ~ 0.693 × (Vc + Vp) / CL = 0.693 × 6.36 / 0.2 ≈ 22 days, consistent class expectations (IgG catabolic half-life 2-4 weeks). SC AUC0-inf = IV AUC0-inf × F1 = IV AUC0-inf × 0.744.
code: typical_nca_conc <- sim_typical |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), Cc > 0) |>   dplyr::mutate(id = paste(treatment, id, sep = "_")) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, Cc, treatment)  typical_nca_dose <- tibble(   id = c("IV_1", "SC_2"),   time = 0,   amt = 140,   treatment = c("IV", "SC") )  nca_typical <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(   PKNCA::PKNCAconc(typical_nca_conc, Cc ~ time | treatment + id),   PKNCA::PKNCAdose(typical_nca_dose, amt ~ time | treatment + id),   intervals = data.frame(     start = 0, end = Inf,     cmax = TRUE, tmax = TRUE, aucinf.obs = TRUE, half.life = TRUE   ) ))  typical_tbl <- as.data.frame(nca_typical$result) |>   dplyr::filter(PPTESTCD %in% c("cmax", "tmax", "aucinf.obs", "half.life")) |>   dplyr::select(treatment, PPTESTCD, PPORRES) |>   tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = PPTESTCD, values_from = PPORRES)  knitr::kable(   typical_tbl,   digits = 3,   caption = "Typical-subject (70 kg, 2 mg/kg) NCA for the Davda 2014 consensus model. The paper does not publish NCA for comparison; values shown for reader inspection." )

title: PK_2cmt_mAb_Davda_2014
text: Davda 2014 tabulate age, sex, race distributions underlying 171 healthy volunteers. virtual cohort uses body weight (truncated normal around 70 kg). population-level demographics flagged NA population metadata rather invented. SC dose administered depot compartment; bioavailability applies (F1 = 0.744). IV dose placed directly central compartment (F = 1). matches model structure Davda 2014. Simulation window (84 days) long enough characterise terminal phase average mAb longer FIH follow-windows original studies. PKNCA comparison table qualitative check : paper publish NCA parameters, side--side published vs simulated table possible. Values tuned target. IIV encoded omega^2 = (CV/100)^2 diagonal, matching paper’s convention reported “%CV” omega × 100 (SD log-normal). -diagonal covariances used reported Table 3.

title: Soehoel_2022_tralokinumab
text: Citation: Soehoel , Larsen MS, Timmermann S. Population Pharmacokinetics Tralokinumab Adult Subjects Moderate Severe Atopic Dermatitis. Clinical Pharmacology Drug Development. 2022;11(8):910-921. doi:10.1002/cpdd.1113 Description: Two-compartment population PK model tralokinumab (Soehoel 2022) adults moderate--severe atopic dermatitis, SC first-order absorption allometric body-weight effects. Article: Clin Pharmacol Drug Dev. 2022;11(8):910-921 (open-access via PMC9796478)

title: Soehoel_2022_tralokinumab
text: published analysis pooled 2561 subjects (13,361 quantifiable serum concentrations) across 10 clinical trials: 3 phase 3 trials (ECZTRA 1/2/3), 4 phase 2 trials (including diluted-formulation trial D2213C00001), 3 phase 1 trials, enrolling atopic-dermatitis patients (n = 2066, 81%), asthma patients (n = 441, 17%), healthy volunteers (n = 54, 2%). Baseline demographics Soehoel 2022 Table 1: age median 38 years (range 18-92, 131 subjects >= 65 years); body weight median 74.5 kg (range 36-165); 55% male / 45% female; race 67% White, 22% Asian, 7% Black/African American; ethnicity 9% Hispanic/Latino. Baseline EASI score AD subjects: median 27.5 (range 12-72). Subcutaneous tralokinumab doses ranged single-dose phase 1 exposures labelled atopic-dermatitis regimen 600 mg SC loading followed 300 mg SC every 2 weeks. information available programmatically via readModelDb("Soehoel_2022_tralokinumab")$population.

title: Soehoel_2022_tralokinumab
text: Every structural parameter, covariate effect, IIV element, residual-error term taken directly Soehoel 2022 Table 2. reference weight 75 kg; nonECZTRA = 0 dilution = 0 define ECZTRA / undiluted reference. Table 2 footnote (d) Soehoel 2022 states IIV reported sqrt(exp(omega^2) - 1), .e., log-normal CV convention. inverse relation omega^2 = log(1 + CV^2) used convert reported 40.1% / 31.3% CVs variance/covariance triple stored ~ c(...) form ini(). previous release stored sqrt(.) values (standard deviations), -estimated IIV simulation time; vignette depends corrected form.

title: Soehoel_2022_tralokinumab
text: Original observed data publicly available. cohort approximates Soehoel 2022 Table 1 demographics restricted adult atopic-dermatitis subpopulation (.e., regimen-relevant population): body weight truncated normal centered reported median (74.5 kg, SD ~18 kg) limits 36-165 kg; 45% female; nonECZTRA = 0 (ECZTRA-trial reference) dilution = 0 (undiluted reference) labelled regimen. Sex race columns retained documentation enter Soehoel 2022 model.
code: set.seed(20260418) n_subj <- 400  cohort <- tibble::tibble(   id        = seq_len(n_subj),   WT        = pmin(pmax(rnorm(n_subj, mean = 74.5, sd = 18), 36), 165),   SEXF      = as.integer(runif(n_subj) < 0.45),   nonECZTRA = 0L,   dilution  = 0L )  # Labelled AD regimen: 600 mg SC loading dose on day 0, 300 mg SC Q2W # (every 14 days) for 12 additional doses => study window 0-182 days. # By doses 10+ the profile is at steady state (terminal half-life ~3 weeks at # typical parameters). load_dose <- 600 maint_dose <- 300 tau <- 14 n_maint <- 12 dose_days <- c(0, seq(tau, tau * n_maint, by = tau)) amt_vec   <- c(load_dose, rep(maint_dose, n_maint))  ev_dose <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(time = dose_days) |>   dplyr::arrange(id, time) |>   dplyr::group_by(id) |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = amt_vec, cmt = "depot", evid = 1L) |>   dplyr::ungroup()  obs_days <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, tau * (n_maint + 1), by = 1),   dose_days + 0.25,   dose_days + 1,   dose_days + 3 )))  ev_obs <- cohort |>   tidyr::crossing(time = obs_days) |>   dplyr::mutate(amt = 0, cmt = NA_character_, evid = 0L)  events <- dplyr::bind_rows(ev_dose, ev_obs) |>   dplyr::arrange(id, time, dplyr::desc(evid)) |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, cmt, evid,                 WT, SEXF, nonECZTRA, dilution)

title: Soehoel_2022_tralokinumab
code: mod <- rxode2::rxode2(readModelDb("Soehoel_2022_tralokinumab")) sim <- rxode2::rxSolve(   mod, events = events,   keep = c("WT", "SEXF", "nonECZTRA", "dilution") )

title: Soehoel_2022_tralokinumab
text: Soehoel 2022 Figure 3 (prediction-corrected VPC) presents 5th/50th/95th percentile prediction bands observed data. Original data publicly available; figure shows simulated 5/50/95 percentile bands virtual AD cohort labelled 600-mg-load--300-mg-Q2W regimen across approximately 13 dosing cycles (steady-state reached cycle 8-10).
code: vpc <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(Cc), time > 0) |>   dplyr::group_by(time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(vpc, aes(time, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.2, fill = "#4682b4") +   geom_line(colour = "#4682b4", linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_vline(xintercept = dose_days, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey70") +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, NA)) +   labs(     x = "Time (days)",     y = expression("Tralokinumab Cc (" * mu * "g/mL)"),     title = "Simulated 5-50-95 percentile VPC; 600 mg SC load then 300 mg SC Q2W",     caption = "Virtual AD cohort (N = 400); WT median 74.5 kg; nonECZTRA = 0, dilution = 0."   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Soehoel_2022_tralokinumab
text: Zoomed-view final Q2W cycle (days 168-182) isolate steady-state peak, trough, AUC_tau used NCA .
code: ss_start <- tau * n_maint       # day 168 (time of dose 13) ss_end   <- ss_start + tau      # day 182  ss_summary <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end, !is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::group_by(time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     Q05 = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     Q50 = quantile(Cc, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),     Q95 = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(ss_summary, aes(time - ss_start, Q50)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q05, ymax = Q95), alpha = 0.2, fill = "#b4464b") +   geom_line(colour = "#b4464b", linewidth = 0.8) +   labs(     x = "Time after final dose (days)",     y = expression("Tralokinumab Cc (" * mu * "g/mL)"),     title = "Steady-state Q2W cycle (dose 13 of 13)",     caption = "Median and 90% prediction interval over one 14-day dosing interval."   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Soehoel_2022_tralokinumab
text: Non-compartmental analysis steady-state Q2W interval (days 168-182). Compute Cmax, Cmin (Ctrough end tau), AUC_tau, average concentration per simulated subject, summarise across cohort.
code: nca_conc <- sim |>   dplyr::filter(time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end, !is.na(Cc)) |>   dplyr::mutate(time_nom = time - ss_start,                 treatment = "300mg_Q2W_SS") |>   dplyr::select(id, time = time_nom, Cc, treatment)  nca_dose <- cohort |>   dplyr::mutate(time = 0, amt = maint_dose, treatment = "300mg_Q2W_SS") |>   dplyr::select(id, time, amt, treatment)  conc_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAconc(nca_conc, Cc ~ time | treatment + id) dose_obj <- PKNCA::PKNCAdose(nca_dose, amt ~ time | treatment + id)  intervals <- data.frame(   start   = 0,   end     = tau,   cmax    = TRUE,   cmin    = TRUE,   auclast = TRUE,   cav     = TRUE )  nca_res <- PKNCA::pk.nca(PKNCA::PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj, intervals = intervals)) summary(nca_res) #>  start end    treatment   N     auclast       cmax       cmin        cav #>      0  14 300mg_Q2W_SS 400 2110 [41.5] 163 [40.7] 130 [43.4] 151 [41.5] #>  #> Caption: auclast, cmax, cmin, cav: geometric mean and geometric coefficient of variation; N: number of subjects

what: Comparison against published typical steady-state exposure
title: Soehoel_2022_tralokinumab
text: Soehoel 2022 tabulate point estimates steady-state Cmax / Cmin / AUC_tau main text; Figure 3 (prediction-corrected VPC) Discussion (Predicting success reduced dosing frequency, 2024 follow-analyses) imply typical steady-state trough concentrations approximately 98 ug/mL typical average concentrations near 130 ug/mL 75-kg adult 600-mg-load + 300-mg-Q2W SC. typical-value (“population-typical”) prediction IIV zeroed provides direct comparison: Typical-subject steady-state exposure (WT = 75 kg, nonECZTRA = 0, dilution = 0; IIV zeroed).
code: mod_typical <- mod |> rxode2::zeroRe()  typical_cohort <- tibble::tibble(   id        = 1L,   WT        = 75,   SEXF      = 0L,   nonECZTRA = 0L,   dilution  = 0L )  ev_typical <- events |>   dplyr::filter(id == 1L) |>   dplyr::mutate(WT = 75)  sim_typical <- rxode2::rxSolve(   mod_typical, events = ev_typical,   keep = c("WT", "SEXF", "nonECZTRA", "dilution") ) |>   as.data.frame() #> ℹ omega/sigma items treated as zero: 'etalvc', 'etalcl'  ss_typical <- sim_typical |>   dplyr::filter(time >= ss_start, time <= ss_end, !is.na(Cc))  typical_summary <- tibble::tibble(   metric = c("Cmax (ug/mL)", "Cmin/Ctrough (ug/mL)",              "Cavg (ug/mL)", "AUC_tau (day*ug/mL)"),   typical_value = c(     max(ss_typical$Cc),     min(ss_typical$Cc),     mean(ss_typical$Cc),     sum(diff(ss_typical$time) *           (ss_typical$Cc[-length(ss_typical$Cc)] +              ss_typical$Cc[-1]) / 2)   ) ) knitr::kable(typical_summary, digits = 2,              caption = "Typical-subject steady-state exposure (WT = 75 kg, nonECZTRA = 0, dilution = 0; IIV zeroed).")

title: Soehoel_2022_tralokinumab
text: pooled-analysis population spans AD, asthma, healthy subjects across 10 trials. virtual cohort restricted AD-relevant reference combination (nonECZTRA = 0, dilution = 0) simulated profile mirrors labelled 600-mg-load + 300-mg-Q2W SC atopic-dermatitis regimen. Body weight drawn truncated normal approximating Table 1 (median 74.5 kg, range 36-165 kg). paper reports overall pooled median range; per-indication WT summary given, approximation unavoidable. Sex retained annotation column; covariate Soehoel 2022 final model. SEXF, nonECZTRA, dilution column names preserve original paper’s mixed-case spellings per nlmixr2lib covariate-columns register model; future models use canonical SEXF / NON_ECZTRA / DILUTION forms. IIV variances back-calculated reported CV% using log-normal convention Table 2 footnote (omega^2 = log(1 + CV^2)). variance-covariance triple passed ini() (0.148971, 0.071977, 0.093459). Soehoel 2022 publish numerical Cmax / Cmin / AUC_tau steady state; PKNCA summary therefore self-consistency check implemented model rather back--paper numerical comparison. Qualitative agreement Figure 3 (prediction-corrected VPC) intended validation. unit conversion required dosing amount (mg) concentration unit (ug/mL) mg / L = ug / mL.

title: Wang_2017_benralizumab
text: Simulate benralizumab concentration-time profiles using final population PK model Wang et al. (2017) healthy volunteers patients asthma (N = 200 across 6 Phase /II studies). Benralizumab humanized, afucosylated IgG1 monoclonal antibody targeting IL-5 receptor alpha. model 2-compartment model first-order SC absorption, allometric weight scaling (CL exponent fixed 0.75), covariate effects high-titer anti-drug antibodies (ADA, titer >= 400) CL Japanese healthy volunteer status Vc. Source: Table 3 Wang et al. (2017) CPT Pharmacometrics Syst Pharmacol. 6(4):249-257. doi:10.1002/psp4.12160. Parameters verified PMC5397562.

title: Wang_2017_benralizumab
code: set.seed(2017) n_subj <- 500  pop <- data.frame(   ID          = seq_len(n_subj),   WT          = rlnorm(n_subj, log(77), 0.24),  # Mean ~77 kg (Table 2)   ADA         = rbinom(n_subj, 1, 0.05),         # ~5% high-titer ADA   JAPANESE_HV = 0                                 # Non-Japanese patient population )

title: Wang_2017_benralizumab
text: Approved asthma regimen: 30 mg SC every 4 weeks first 3 doses, every 8 weeks. Simulate 30 weeks.
code: dose_times <- c(0, 28, 56, 112, 168)  # days (weeks 0, 4, 8, 16, 24)  obs_times <- sort(unique(c(   seq(0, 7, by = 0.5),   seq(7, 210, by = 3.5) )))  d_dose <- pop %>%   crossing(TIME = dose_times) %>%   mutate(AMT = 30, EVID = 1, CMT = 1, DV = NA_real_)  d_obs <- pop %>%   crossing(TIME = obs_times) %>%   mutate(AMT = NA_real_, EVID = 0, CMT = 2, DV = NA_real_)  d_sim <- bind_rows(d_dose, d_obs) %>%   arrange(ID, TIME, desc(EVID)) %>%   as.data.frame()

title: Wang_2017_benralizumab
code: mod <- readModelDb("Wang_2017_benralizumab") sim <- rxSolve(mod, d_sim, returnType = "data.frame") #> ℹ parameter labels from comments will be replaced by 'label()'

title: Wang_2017_benralizumab
code: sim_summary <- sim %>%   filter(time > 0) %>%   group_by(time) %>%   summarise(     median = median(Cc, na.rm = TRUE),     lo     = quantile(Cc, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),     hi     = quantile(Cc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(sim_summary, aes(x = time / 7)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.2, fill = "darkorange") +   geom_line(aes(y = median), color = "darkorange", linewidth = 1) +   labs(     x = "Time (weeks)",     y = "Benralizumab concentration (mg/L)",     title = "Simulated benralizumab PK (30 mg SC, q4w x3 then q8w)",     subtitle = "Median and 90% prediction interval (N = 500 virtual asthma patients)",     caption = "Model: Wang et al. (2017) CPT Pharmacometrics Syst Pharmacol"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Wang_2017_benralizumab
text: NCA summary (2nd dosing interval, weeks 4-8)
code: # Use 2nd dosing interval (weeks 4-8) sim_df <- as.data.frame(sim) # Build a unique subject key: use sim.id (replicate) and id (original subject) # when both are available; otherwise fall back to whichever exists if (all(c("sim.id", "id") %in% names(sim_df))) {   sim_df$subject <- paste(sim_df$sim.id, sim_df$id, sep = "_") } else if ("id" %in% names(sim_df)) {   sim_df$subject <- sim_df$id } else {   sim_df$subject <- sim_df$sim.id } nca_data <- data.frame(   subject  = sim_df$subject,   time_rel = sim_df$time - 28,   Cc       = sim_df$Cc ) nca_data <- nca_data[nca_data$time_rel >= 0 & nca_data$time_rel <= 28 & nca_data$Cc > 0, ]  conc_obj <- PKNCAconc(nca_data, Cc ~ time_rel | subject) dose_obj <- PKNCAdose(   data.frame(subject = unique(nca_data$subject), time_rel = 0, AMT = 30),   AMT ~ time_rel | subject ) data_obj <- PKNCAdata(conc_obj, dose_obj,                        intervals = data.frame(start = 0, end = 28,                                               cmax = TRUE, tmax = TRUE,                                               auclast = TRUE, half.life = TRUE)) nca_results <- pk.nca(data_obj) #>  ■■■■■■■                           18% |  ETA:  5s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■              63% |  ETA:  2s nca_summary <- summary(nca_results) knitr::kable(nca_summary, digits = 2,              caption = "NCA summary (2nd dosing interval, weeks 4-8)")

title: Wang_2017_benralizumab
text: Model: 2-compartment SC linear elimination. TMDD. Bioavailability: 52.6% SC (estimated, fixed). Allometry: CL exponent fixed 0.75 (standard); Vc Vp exponents estimated 0.651 0.576 respectively. ADA effect: High-titer ADA (>= 400) causes exp(1.52) = 4.57-fold increase CL (Eq. 6 paper; Table 3 footnote c “natural exponent”). ADA time-varying (assessed sampling visit). Japanese race: 1.34-fold larger Vc Japanese healthy volunteers (multiplicative factor; may confound healthy volunteer status). IIV 6 PK parameters (CL, Vc, Q, Vp, ka, F). Reference weight: 77 kg (population mean; paper states “normalized population median” exact median explicitly stated).
